title: A Harrowing Race Against Time to Find a Downed U.S. Airman in Iran
summary: For the Iranians, the Air Force colonel whose fighter jet had been shot down was possible leverage. For the U.S. military, finding him was a moral imperative.

title: Trump Revels in Threats to Commit War Crimes in Iran
summary: The president said he would bomb Iran “back to the Stone Ages.” Until this administration, American leaders had insisted they were trying to follow international law in war.

title: This Is Not China’s War, but Beijing Started Preparing for It Years Ago
summary: Long concerned about geopolitical crises, China redoubled efforts to secure energy security when President Trump started raising the stakes in his first term.

title: Stephen Miller Is Still Pursuing His Immigration Agenda, but More Quietly
summary: The architect of President Trump’s mass deportation campaign wants “a moratorium on immigration from third world countries until we can heal ourselves as a nation.” The chaos in Minneapolis has not pushed him off that course.

title: ICE Agents Detain Newlywed Spouse of Soldier Training to Deploy
summary: The 22-year-old wife of an Army staff sergeant came to the U.S. as a toddler. She was taken from a military base where the couple planned to live.

title: Houston Cheers on Artemis II Moon Mission, Reclaiming Its Place as ‘Space City’
summary: The Artemis II mission elicited deep feelings for many Americans, particularly in Houston, the home of mission control.

title: NASA Artemis II Astronauts Race Into Moon’s Embrace After Quiet Easter
summary: Ahead of a lunar flyby on Monday, the crew celebrated the astronaut Jeremy Hansen’s first spaceflight and got a special message from Charlie Duke, the Apollo 16 moonwalker.

title: Artemis II Moon Flyby: Crew, Timeline and What to Know
summary: The journey around the moon of three Americans and one Canadian is going into its sixth day, but it’s not too late to get caught up on it.

title: China Mourned an Education Influencer. The Grief Was a Quiet Revolt.
summary: Zhang Xuefeng helped people navigate the country’s unforgiving higher education system. The public outpouring after his death was a quiet rebuke to the punishing process.

title: A Food Pantry Network Suddenly Shuttered, Leaving Thousands Scrambling
summary: Ruby’s Pantry had 85 locations in communities in Minnesota, Wisconsin, North Dakota and Iowa.

title: Renovate or Replace: The Fight Over How to Fix N.Y.C. Public Housing
summary: The city thinks four developments in Chelsea are too run-down to be saved, and wants to rebuild them, adding mixed-income housing. Some residents are opposed.

title: New Chef Program Helps People With Autism Find Jobs in Fine-Dining Restaurants
summary: Culinary jobs have the potential to be a perfect fit, and a new effort is afoot to help autistic workers land them.

title: The War Is Turning Iran Into a Major World Power
summary: Its newfound might derives from its control the Strait of Hormuz.

title: The Old Way of Campaigning Won’t Cut It Anymore
summary: Is this a great way to audition and select our leaders, especially for executive offices? Not particularly.

title: New Owner of Shopping Plaza in Kansas City Pitches a $1.5 Billion Rescue Plan
summary: The Country Club Plaza’s sale and turnaround plans come at a consequential economic time for Kansas City, Mo., which will soon lose the Chiefs football team.

title: Pepsi Drops Sponsorship of Wireless Festival Headlined by Kanye West
summary: Prime Minister Keir Starmer said he was “deeply concerned” that the rapper known for antisemitic and racist comments had been booked to perform at the Wireless Festival.

title: 3 Dead After High Winds Topple Tree During Easter Egg Hunt in Germany
summary: A 21-year-old woman, her baby and a 16-year-old girl were killed after a nearly 100-foot tree fell in a wooded area in northern Germany, the police said.

title: Former Matador Is Gored to Death Before Annual Bullfight in Spain
summary: The accident happened before the Corrida Picassiana, an annual event in Malaga that honors the painter Pablo Picasso.
